summary: Patrick reports his best month ever for Bingo Card Creator, earning $918.25 gross and $756.35 net profit from 36 sales in June 2007. Despite summer vacation reducing teacher-related search traffic, he grew overall traffic and sales by rewriting a single page to capture long-tail 'snowflake' search queries. The rewritten page doubled its pageviews from 12% to 27% of total site traffic. He attributes his sales growth directly to this SEO content strategy and is eager to expand it further.
key_points: Best month ever with $756.35 net profit on 36 sales despite being in the seasonal low period for his teacher-focused product
A single page rewrite targeting long-tail 'snowflake' queries generated 3,000 additional pageviews, offsetting seasonal traffic decline
Trial-to-purchase conversion held at ~2.5%, with 9% of visitors who reached the purchasing page completing a purchase
Total expenses remained low at $161.90, with AdWords and SwiftCD being the largest costs
money_quotes: Easily my best month ever, and its summer vacation. I'm really looking forward to the start of the term in August.

The "extra" 3,000 page views were a major part of the reason that, despite the fact traffic on my major teacher-related search terms declined since teachers are out of school, my overall traffic stayed mostly flat while my sales increased markedly.
I can't wait to get some time to write some more content and catch myself some more snowflakes.
key_insight: A single page rewrite targeting long-tail search queries can offset seasonal traffic declines and drive meaningful sales growth for a small software business.
